metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5169
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1H-L: confocal imaging of immunofluorescence labeling for CRIP1a (CNRIP1) and CB1R (CNR1) in showed immunoreactivity for both throughout the hippocampus. However, the subcellular distribution of CRIP1a and CB1R proteins only partly overlapped.
"While CB1R staining was restricted to terminal axon fibers and endings, CRIP1a immunoreactivity was found in somata or widely distributed in the surrounding neuropil (Fig. 1i), but also in intimate contact (CA1 stratum pyramidale; Fig. 1j) or direct overlap (CA1 stratum radiatum; Fig. 1k, l) with CB1R-expressing processes. These data suggest that CB1Rs are in close proximity to CRIP1a. CRIP1a immunostaining was abolished by pre-absorption of antisera with the CRIP1a antigen peptide, confirming antibody specificity (Keimpema et al. 2010)."

Figure 2: higher resolution confocal imaging of immunofluorescence labeling for CRIP1a (CNRIP1), VGluT1 (to mark excitatory presynapse), GAD (to mark inhibitory presynapse) and DAGLα (enzyme diacylglycerol lipase-α, produces CB1R ligand 2-arachidonoylglycerol, localized to postsynapse). CRIP1a partially co-localized with both presynaptic but not the postsynaptic markers.

authors conclude:
"Together, these data strongly suggest the predominantly presynaptic localization of CRIP1a within the synaptic domain.". Experimental description: M&M: "Adult (3-6 months old) male mice were used for all experiments."

antibody:
anti-CRIP1a; 1:500, (Keimpema et al. 2010) PMID:20962221

"CRIP1a immunostaining was abolished by pre-absorption of antisera with the CRIP1a antigen peptide, confirming antibody specificity (Keimpema et al. 2010)."
